title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: So I'm going to take you to all the biggest sites and attractions that the city has to offer.
Now I'm going to dig into my bagel.
This looks like it's going to be the shortest line that we've had to stand in for food so far, but looks can be deceiving.
Tomorrow, I'm going to see Wicked.

title: will / shall
examples: I think kids will love this bagel.
Get the hell out of here.
I am at Magnolia Bakery, which is well known for its banana pudding.
I'll just do a small almond milk latte, please.
You can feel the energy when you come here, but I will say you need to be a little bit careful in Times Square.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: You can feel the energy when you come here, but I will say you need to be a little bit careful in Times Square.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: Can I pick that?
In the summer they have concerts and in winter you can go ice skating here which I did years ago and it was lots of fun.
You could go to one.
Today it is very foggy so we can't see anything.
I'm kind of bummed because normally when the weather is nice, you can see all of the famous landmarks like the Empire State Building.

title: have to / must
examples: So I'm going to take you to all the biggest sites and attractions that the city has to offer.
This looks like it's going to be the shortest line that we've had to stand in for food so far, but looks can be deceiving.
I guess Koreans must like very strong coffee.
In my opinion, if you only have time to do one thing while you're here, you must go see a Broadway show.

title: should (advice)
examples: He should be the one carrying this, huh?
I'm not usually a huge meat eater, so this should be interesting.
The amount of cheese and meats on here should be criminal.
Plus, I think when it's your first time trying something, you always should go for the classic.

title: had better / it's time
examples: It's been a long day of walking out in the cold, so it's time for some sugar and coffee.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: How much would it pay?
I would compare it to Song Su in Seoul because it looks a little rough on the outside, but it's got very trendy cafes and restaurants and lots of cool events going on.
I think it's delicious, but if I ate too much of it, I think I would definitely get a stomach ache.
I'd say today was a big success.
Let me know in the comments what you would like to do most in New York City.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: If you go to Liberty Bagels I do recommend this because it's the most popular menu item and it's very good.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: I don't think the bagel is flavored at all.
Now we are in Dumbo, which is located in Brooklyn.

title: have something done
examples: Get the hell out of here.
